varieties.name: Kohala
varieties.origin: Hawaii, USA (introduced from Southeast Asia, 1954 to Florida)
varieties.traits: Large fruit (10-12.5g average), medium-sized seed, light thin yellowish-brown skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Aromatic, sweet, spicy flavor. Translucent white flesh, soft to medium texture. High aril recovery (60-70%)
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable blooming and fruiting. Mean yield 15.7 kg/tree. Early season (January-February in Australia)

varieties.name: Biew Kiew (Biew Khiew)
varieties.traits: Large fruit (average 10-11g, can reach 18g), lopsided shape, thick yellowish-brown skin when ripe, small seed
varieties.flesh_fruit: Intense sweetness with strong caramel/butterscotch flavor and floral aroma. Thick, crisp, firm texture. Very sweet with aromatic profile. High quality.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy consistent cropper. Maximum yield 72kg on 9-year tree recorded. Mean yield 23.4 kg/tree. Late season (March-April in Australia)
varieties.availability: Available from Queensland nurseries: Daley's Fruit, The Thorny Fruit Co (Mareeba favorite), specialized tropical fruit nurseries

varieties.name: Haew (How/Chompoo)
varieties.traits: Medium to large fruit (15-18g), consistently round shape, slightly thicker skin than other varieties, medium seed
varieties.flesh_fruit: Notable sweetness with fragrant aroma. Good quality, crisp texture. Popular canning variety in Thailand.
varieties.yield_notes: Late maturing cultivar. Mean yield 8.3 kg/tree. Tends to alternate bearing (on/off years). Heavy cropper in good years.
varieties.availability: Available at The Longan Farm Narangba, Daley's Fruit, humid subtropical climates area fruit tree nurseries

varieties.name: Homestead
varieties.traits: Medium to large fruit, upright vigorous tree growth
varieties.flesh_fruit: Acceptable eating quality, sweet flavor, soft texture. Good quality but rated slightly lower than late-season Thai types.
varieties.yield_notes: Mean yield 8.7 kg/tree. Early season variety (late January-early February). Reliable bearer.

varieties.name: Kay Sweeney (KSweeney)
varieties.traits: Very large fruit (12.5g average - largest of tested varieties), slightly bigger than parent Kohala. Medium/large seed.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Soft, sweeter flesh. High flesh recovery at 72.2% (highest percentage). Good quality.
varieties.yield_notes: Early season (late January-early February). Consistent bearer. Only cultivar with significant fruit in largest size category (25-32mm).

varieties.name: Diamond River
varieties.traits: Medium fruit size, susceptible to limb breakage under heavy crop load
varieties.flesh_fruit: Award-winning exceptional flavor profile. Fair to good quality. Sweet taste.
varieties.yield_notes: Fruits every year (no alternate bearing). Precocious - bears early. Produces off-season crops. Late season producer.
varieties.availability: Available from specialized tropical fruit nurseries in Queensland

varieties.name: Daw (E-Daw/Edaw)
varieties.origin: Thailand (73% of Thai longan area, most popular Thai cultivar)
varieties.traits: Large fruit with big seed (15-18g fruit weight), thin skin, thick flesh, greenish new growth. Low aril recovery due to large seed.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet with good flavor, fairly crisp but not as crispy as Biew Kiew. Thick aromatic flesh. Excellent quality for early season.
varieties.yield_notes: Early maturing (name means 'early'). Consistent bearer, no alternate bearing problems. Low yield in some locations (1.5 kg/tree in Australia trials). Fruits don't store well on tree - seeds may germinate in fruit.
varieties.availability: Limited availability in Australian nurseries, some Queensland specialty suppliers

varieties.name: Dang (E-Dang/Red Stem)
varieties.traits: Medium sized fruit, red new stem growth (hence 'Red Stem')
varieties.flesh_fruit: Fair quality, sweet but not particularly crisp. Acceptable eating quality.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy-bearing variety. Mean yield 12.7 kg/tree. Late season (mid-July to early August in Thailand; late January-February in Australia).
varieties.availability: Limited in Australian market, some Queensland tropical fruit specialists

varieties.name: Sri Chompoo (Pink Flesh/Chompoo)
varieties.traits: Large fruit, greenish-brown to reddish skin when ripe, small seed, red/pink new growth, pink-tinted flesh
varieties.flesh_fruit: Faint pink tinge in flesh ('Chompoo' = pink in Thai). High quality, sweet with pleasant aroma. Very ornamental foliage.
varieties.yield_notes: Maximum yield 76 kg on 6-year tree (highest recorded). Mean yield 25.7 kg/tree (highest average). Late season (March-April). Not preferred for canning due to pink flesh color.

varieties.name: Ilao
varieties.traits: Medium fruit size, early-type variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Flavor and eating quality similar to late Thai types. Good quality. High percentage parthenocarpic fruit in first season.
varieties.yield_notes: Early type fruiting. Under evaluation with promising results.

varieties.name: Duan Yu
varieties.traits: Medium sized fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Poor fruit quality - texture and flavor not recommended
varieties.yield_notes: Mid-season type (mid-February). Mean yield 16.2 kg/tree.

varieties.name: Pon Yai
varieties.traits: Small fruit size, susceptible to pink wax scale and sooty mold
varieties.flesh_fruit: Poor quality
varieties.yield_notes: Very poor - lowest yields recorded at 1.7 kg/tree. Mid-season (mid-February).

varieties.name: Shixia 🏆
varieties.origin: Guangdong Province, China - 140+ year history (heirloom variety established ~1880s)
varieties.traits: Small fruit (12-15g average) but exceptionally high quality. Dense foliage, medium tree height ideal for high-density planting.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Crisp, thick, very sweet aril. Extremely high TSS (Total Soluble Solids) at 19-20%. Best cultivar for consuming fresh. Excellent flavor - superior quality.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy crops regularly on unusually large panicles. High-density system yields 2,250 kg per mu on 8-year trees (120 trees/mu). Most cultivated in Guangdong province.
varieties.availability: Available from tropical monsoon climates specialty nurseries, Greens of tropical monsoon climates, Indian agricultural research stations

varieties.name: Chuliang 🏆
varieties.origin: Gaozhou area, Guangdong Province, China (native superior cultivar, long established heritage)
varieties.traits: Large fruit (12-16.5g average), thick skin. Currently covers over 100,000 ha in China (30,000 ha in Gaozhou alone).
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick, firm aril. Very sweet (TSS 20-23%) with fragrant flavor. High aril recovery (69-74% edible portion). Golden yellow when dried. Excellent drying variety.
varieties.yield_notes: High and stable yield. Output over 60,000 metric tons from 12,000 ha production area. Early bearing, hereditary stability.
varieties.availability: Greens of tropical monsoon climates, tropical monsoon climates agricultural departments, Indian tropical fruit nurseries

varieties.name: Wulongling (Black Dragon Peak) 🏆
varieties.origin: Fujian Province, China - 150+ year history (major heirloom cultivar established ~1870s)

varieties.name: E-Daw/Yiduo (Edaw) 🌱
varieties.origin: Thailand (tropical ecotype, major export variety - 90% of Thai longan imports to China)
varieties.traits: Coarse large fruit, thick flesh, small seed, greenish new growth. Vigorous growth, easy flowering.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Exceptionally sweet, thick aromatic flesh. Crisp, juicy texture. High edible rate. Very high quality.
varieties.yield_notes: Extremely high - production can reach 2,000-3,000 kg per mu in Thailand. Year-round production possible in Vietnam. Most exported longan variety.

varieties.name: Ruby Longan
varieties.origin: Modern selection/hybrid from tropical breeding programs
varieties.traits: Ruby red color of fruits and leaves (eye-catching ornamental value), evergreen
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very juicy and sweet with ruby red color. Translucent flesh with grape-like consistency. Taste resembling lychee and grape. Considered superfood quality.
varieties.yield_notes: Fruits in 2-3 years from planting. Good productivity.

varieties.name: Diamond River (Belian) 🧬
varieties.origin: Thailand (recent introduction, widely tested in tropical regions)
varieties.traits: Medium fruit, susceptible to limb breakage, precocious
varieties.flesh_fruit: Award-winning exceptional flavor. Fair to good quality, thick flesh, small seed.
varieties.yield_notes: Fruits every year without alternate bearing. Off-season production capability. Early bearing. Reported to fruit annually.

varieties.name: Pingpong 🌱
varieties.traits: Compact growth habit, suitable for small gardens and container growing
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, aromatic. Good quality for fresh eating.
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields for tree size. Regular bearer.

varieties.name: Fuyan (Lucky Eye) 🏆
varieties.origin: Fujian Province, China (major ancient cultivar, accounts for 90%+ of Fujian plantings)
varieties.traits: Large fruit (18g average), thin skin, small seed. Over one million trees in cultivation (long history).
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick crisp aril. Lower TSS (15-16%) than fresh varieties but excellent for canning. High quality processed product.
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding. Best cultivar for canning industry.

varieties.name: Wu Yuan (Wuyuan/Black Round) 🌱
varieties.origin: Guangdong Province, China (important traditional cultivar, introduced to USA in 1930)
varieties.traits: Medium fruit (15g), large seed. Vigorous tree growth. Used extensively as rootstock for its vigor.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Soft and juicy aril. Average quality for fresh but excellent for drying. TSS 14-15%. More acidic than other varieties.
varieties.yield_notes: High yield. Traditional canning variety. Extensively used for vegetative propagation (inarching).

varieties.name: Fengko (Fukien/Fenke) 🌱
varieties.origin: Fujian Province, China (introduced to Taiwan 300+ years ago, now 95%+ of Taiwan production)

varieties.name: Lidongben 🏆
varieties.origin: Fujian Province, China (traditional cultivar, major regional variety)

varieties.name: Songfengben 🌱
varieties.traits: Medium fruit. Highly sensitive to potassium chlorate flowering induction (90%+ flowering rates).
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality. Lowest quercetin content (69.6 µg/g) but still nutritionally valuable.
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent response to KClO3 treatment allows controlled flowering - can achieve 90%+ flowering rates with proper treatment.

varieties.name: Dawuyuan 🌱
varieties.traits: Medium to large fruit. Well-adapted to Guangxi tropical/subtropical conditions.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality, sweet flavor suitable for fresh consumption.

varieties.name: Biew Kiew 🌱
varieties.traits: Large fruit (10-18g), lopsided shape, small seed, thick skin (longer shelf life). Dark green leaves, red new growth. One of 4 varieties suitable for Malaysia.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Intense sweetness, strong caramel flavor, floral aroma. Thick crisp firm texture. Highest quality Thai variety. Thick flesh, small seed ratio.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy consistent yields. High productivity. Most widely grown for Thai commercial production.

varieties.name: Sri Chompoo (Si Chompoo) 🌱
varieties.traits: Large attractive fruit, rose-tinted/pink color, greenish-brown to reddish skin, small seed. Red/pink new foliage (ornamental).
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pink-tinted flesh (unique). Mouthwatering sweetness, pleasant aromatic fragrance. Premium quality. Very ornamental.
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity. Mid-season. Highly responsive to KClO3 treatment (100% flowering at just 1 g/m²).

varieties.name: Haew (Water Chestnut) 🌱
varieties.traits: Medium to large fruit (15-18g), consistently round, slightly thicker skin, medium seed
varieties.flesh_fruit: Notable sweetness, fragrant aroma. Crisp quality. Popular canning variety. Good quality.
varieties.yield_notes: Popular in Thai markets. Late maturing. Pronounced alternate bearing habit. Good yields in 'on' years.

varieties.name: Baidum (Black Leaf) 🌱
varieties.traits: Dark foliage. Late season variety (late August-September harvest in Thailand).
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality, valued in Thai homeland.
varieties.yield_notes: Late season extends harvest period. Good productivity.

varieties.name: Vietnamese Longan var. longepetiolulatus
varieties.origin: Southern Vietnam (indigenous tropical ecotype, distinct from Chinese varieties)
varieties.traits: True tropical longan adapted to pure monsoonal conditions. Can yield 2-3 crops per year. Smooth yellowish-tan fruit.
varieties.flesh_fruit: White to off-white or pinkish translucent flesh. Sweet juicy quality.
varieties.yield_notes: Multiple crops possible (2-3 per year in pure tropical). Year-round availability in Vietnam. Production areas: Mekong Delta, southern provinces.

varieties.name: Xuong Com Vang (XCV)
varieties.traits: Vietnamese selection adapted to Mekong Delta conditions
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality for tropical monsoon regions
varieties.yield_notes: Requires 24-36 g/m KClO3 for flowering induction. Cultivated commercially in Vietnam.

varieties.name: Tieu Da Bo (TDB)
varieties.traits: Vietnamese selection for monsoon production
varieties.flesh_fruit: Quality suitable for fresh market and processing
varieties.yield_notes: Requires 20-40 g/m KClO3 treatment. Commercial cultivation in Vietnam.

varieties.name: Longnhan
varieties.traits: High bearing capacity variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent - noted specifically for high bearing capacity. Choice variety for postharvest processing.

varieties.name: Tieuhue
varieties.traits: Traditional Vietnamese genetics
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality for tropical regions
varieties.yield_notes: Choicest variety for postharvest processing in Asia-Pacific region.
